.. currentmodule:: gmaopy.stats.statistics

The Statistics class
--------------------

.. class:: Statistics(object)

    .. attribute:: missing_value = 1.7E38

        The official missing value used by SemperPy for statistics

    .. automethod:: register

        :param category: the are different groups of statistics for different applications, to date, there is *obsstat* for observation (impact) statistics, *score* for forecast skill scores, *product* for statistics on analyses
        :type category: str
        :param name: name of the statistic. This is the official name which will be used by the user
        :type name: string
        :param what: A class to instanciate to implement the statistics named *name*
        :type what: class

        Registers classes to implement different statistics. Once a class is registered, its name
        is known by the system and it can be stored in a database.

        :note: class method

    .. automethod:: create
        
        :param category: the category under which we are working,
        :type category: str
        :param name: name of the statistic to instanticiate
        :type name: string

        Instanciates the class corresponding to that name in the given category. 
        If an instance of that class has been created earlier, it is returned (singleton), 
        this speeds up the process.

        :warning: statistics classes are not supposed to store attributes.

        :note: class method


    .. automethod:: data2np

        :param data: the data as passed to the compute method
        :type data: list
        :param index: index in the tuple
        :type index: int

        The data is sent to the compute methods as represented by the SQL client, it is a list of 
        tuple, each tuple contains the value of each column described in the select statement. By default,
        this tuple is: level,variable,statistic,date,value,count,expver.

        This method returns a numpy array containing all the values in the list of value number *index*
        in the tuple. It slices data vertically, for example::

            data = [
                (500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,4.5,34,'expver1'),
                (500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,40.5,134,'expver2'),
                (500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,14.5,354,'expver3'),
                (500.0,'t','sum',2012010100,379.5,2500,'expver3'),
            ]
            print s.data2np(data,4)
            numpy array: [4.5,40.5,14.5,379.5]

        :note: utility method

    .. automethod:: split

        :param data: the data as passed to the compute method
        :type data: list
        :param index: index in the tuple
        :type index: int

        This method splits the list of data into as many lists as different values of column number *index*
        is found and returns a dictionary with each list. For example::

            data = [
                (500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,4.5,34,'expver1'),
                (500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,40.5,134,'expver2'),
                (500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,14.5,354,'expver3'),
                (500.0,'t','sum',2012010100,379.5,2500,'expver3'),
            ]
            print s.splitData(data,6)
            {
                'expver1': [(500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,4.5,34,'expver1')],
                'expver2': [(500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,40.5,134,'expver2')],
                'expver3': [(500.0,'h','sum',2012010100,14.5,354,'expver3'),
                            (500.0,'t','sum',2012010100,379.5,2500,'expver3')]
            }

        This method can be called as many times as needed on results from previous call

        :note: utility method

    .. automethod:: store

        :param storer: the storer passed to the compute method
        :type storer: hidden
        :param record: the record data passed to the compute method
        :type record: dict
        :param kargs: values to insert in the record before storing it

        This method is also a utility function, it stores data::

            s.stor(storer,record,value=24.3,count=2000)

        :note: utility method

    .. automethod:: array

        :param value: the value to store in an array
        :type value: float

        This utility method builds a masked array of one value, this is useful to return the 
        final floating point value after aggregating statistics from a database.

        :note: utility method

    .. automethod:: requirements_raw

        This method must return the name of a variable to be extracted from the raw file,
        variable for gridded field or variable for ODS files. By default it returns
        ['var'] which means *whichever variable the user specified*. However, sometimes it
        can get a little more complicated. To compute the normalized cost we need whichever
        variable the user specified (oma or omf) and xvec, in that case this method should::

            def requirements_raw(self):
                return ['var','xvec']

        :note: information method

        To compute the estimared sigo, we want to ignore the variable the user specified
        because we need two variables, oma and omf. In that case this method should::

            def requirements_raw(self):
                    return ['oma','omf']
    
    .. automethod:: requirements_db

        This method is only used when computing statistics from the database. By 
        default it returns the name of the statistic computed by this object.
        The final name of a statistic (e.g. rms) is often different from the name of the
        statistic stored in the database which is used to compute it (e.g. sumsquare).
        This method should return the name of the statistic to fetch from the database. It can also
        return the name of statistics for a particular variable (e.g. ['sumsquare:omf'].
        If you specify that you want to compute the root mean square (rms), the system uses this method
        to determine which statistic(s) from raw data to generate. When plotting, the system calls this
        method to determine which partial statistic(s) to retrieve from the database, for rms::

            def requirements_db():
                return 'sumsquare'

        :note: information method

    .. automethod:: unit

        This method must either return None (no specific unit) or a unit for that statistic. By default
        None is returned and the variable is used to determine the unit (e.g. rms of meters is meters).
        If the statistic has a unit, like for example a coefficient (%), it should be returned here and it
        will be used instead of the unit from the variable.

        :note: information method

    .. automethod:: moreIsBetter

        This method is needed when plotting to determine sometimes how data needs to be presented.
        By default it return False (e.g. rms, the higher the worse). This enables to orient colorbars
        in a consistent way.

        :note: information method

    .. automethod:: columns

        This method returns an empty list by default. If a statistic requires more columns from the 
        database than returned by default, it can add here the extra required column names.

        :note: information method

    ..automethod:: prepareProcessing

        :param what: the name of the plot being processed (its class)
        :type what: str

        This method is called once only at the very beginning of computing
        statistics to give objects a chance to initialise data, or open
        files etc...

    ..automethod:: completeProcessing

        :param what: the name of the plot being processed (its class)
        :type what: str

        This method is called once only at the very end of computing
        statistics to give objects a chance to close files, normalize
        data etc...

    .. method:: name()
    
        This method must return the official name of this statistic

        :note: abstract method must be overwritten

    .. method:: compute_raw(variables,name,storer,record)

        :param variables: a dictionary of masked arrays for each variable requested for that statistic
        :type variables: dict
        :param name: name of the variable which was specified by the user
        :type name: str
        :param storer: an object in charge of storing data
        :param record: a record containing all the information about the statistic we are about to compute
        :type record: dict

        This method is called when the data specified by the user has been extracted from the raw data. At
        this stage, the variable *record* was filled with all the details to describe the piece of data
        (expver, date, level, etc...), this method is supposed to compute and fill in two keywords:
            
            - value
            - count (could be optional)

        Please see examples in :ref:`makeyourownstat` for more information.

    .. method:: compute_db(columns,data,name)

        :param columns: a dictionary indexed by the name of columns form the database, containing an index in the tuple representing the data
        :type columns: dict
        :param data: A dictionanry of list of tuples containing data from the database, please see :func:`data2np`
        :type data: dict
        :param name: name of the variable which was specified by the user
        :type name: str

        This method is called when the data specified by the user has been extracted from database and
        aggregated according to the user's specifications.

        Please see examples in :ref:`makeyourownstat` for more information.
